project_name: Evidence-First Context Ladder Prompt Layout Ablation

decision.recommended_next_action: Adopt the evidence-first context-ladder layout as the next default prompt layout and validate it on a second repo or larger seed sweep without creating another generic successor branch.
decision.stop_reason: The focused layout ablation produced durable code, tests, deterministic controls, and local LLM evidence that fine-evidence-first ordering beats summary-leading ladder prompts under the same selected context.

recommended_next_action: Adopt the evidence-first context-ladder layout as the next default prompt layout and validate it on a second repo or larger seed sweep without creating another generic successor branch.
run_notes_tail: # Run Notes
## 2026-04-18 initial execution plan
- Reviewed this project directory: it is a routing/branch shell with prompts and OMX metadata only; no executable harness artifacts are present yet.
- Acquire the parent project harness and results into this project rather than creating another branch.

- Implement a focused prompt-layout ablation that keeps context-ladder selection unchanged while varying generated prompt layout: summary-leading baseline vs fine-evidence-first and/or summary-demoted variants.
- Run smoke/unit validation and then the same local repo-QA LLM benchmark (or a calibrated pilot if full inference is unavailable) with durable metrics.
- Update run notes, result artifacts, and `.omx/project_decision.json` with the evidence and next action.

Branch-specific kill condition: stop this mechanism as unsupported if evidence-first/summary-demoted layouts do not improve exact-match/contains accuracy or reduce summary-bias failures versus the parent summary-leading ladder by at least one repo-QA item without increasing context overrun/failures on the same benchmark.
## 2026-04-18 implementation and benchmark results
- Copied the parent context-ladder repo-QA harness into this project (`src/`, `tests/`) and kept parent outputs under `results/parent_baseline_`.
- Implemented layout-only ladder variants:
- `context_ladder_evidence_first`: same selected ladder items, chunks before summaries.
- `context_ladder_summary_demoted`: same selected ladder items, chunks first plus prompt text that labels summaries as background.

- Added regression tests proving the evidence-first variant preserves item membership/token count while reordering prompt layout, and that summary-demoted prompts label fine evidence before demoted summaries.
- Verification: `.venv/bin/pytest -q` passed (`6 passed`).

- Deterministic repo-QA control: `results/ablation_deterministic/repo_qa_summary.md`; all ladder layouts matched exactly on evidence recall/F1 because selection is unchanged and the extractive reader is order-insensitive.
- Local LLM ablation: `results/ablation_llm/repo_qa_llm_results.json` and `results/ablation_analysis.md` using Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, seed 344, 12 repo-QA items, budgets 600/900/1200.
- Evidence-first vs summary-leading generated-answer F1 deltas: +0.299 at budget 600, +0.060 at 900, +0.226 at 1200. Wins/ties/losses across budget-query pairs: 14/17/5.
- Summary-demoted was mixed: +0.154 at 600, -0.089 at 900, +0.175 at 1200; wins/ties/losses 14/13/9.
- Kill condition not met: evidence-first improved at least one repo-QA item and improved mean F1 at all tested budgets without changing token count or evidence recall.

- Performance note: full LLM run took ~200.1s for 144 generations after cached model load. Pre/post UMA memory showed ~116 GiB available; idle GPU samples were 0% utilization, so sampled utilization is too coarse to prove saturation.
Conclusion: evidence-first ordering is the branch-specific mechanism to carry forward; summary-demotion prose is less reliable and should not be the default.

- Post-analysis cleanup: updated `src/repo_qa_llm_benchmark.py` summary text so future runs report evidence-first and summary-demoted deltas against summary-leading context ladder, then re-ran `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile src/*.py` and `.venv/bin/pytest -q` successfully.
